Liane of the Vara house composed the nocturne.

transcript.text: [The flooded archive room settles under a thin, gray light; warped cases stand open, and a covered music desk waits at center stage. Water ticks somewhere out of sight while the conservatory hall holds its breath around the unsigned nocturne.]

phase.name: The Verdict of Style
phase.intent: To let the room begin in certainty before any material proof has entered it.

transcript.text: Before a name, what can the room itself safely give us: an unsigned nocturne, found here in the conservatory archive, under Vess's roof? What is fact, and what is only reputation beginning to whisper?

deliberation.diagnosis: With fewer than two prior figures, no figure rut is possible.

transcript.text: The nocturne is unsigned and was found here — those are the only facts the room gives me outright. That Vess presides over this conservatory is also grounded. But presiding is not composing. The rules demand ink, paper, ledger entries, and a correcting hand; reputation gives me none of those.

meta.hypothesis: Vess composed the nocturne — but this is reputation whispering, not evidence speaking. Not yet grounded.

transcript.text: [The cover remains over the music desk as damp air gathers at the room's high windows. A clerk sets a dry blotter beside the waiting leaves, then withdraws without opening them.]
Good: you have separated the room from the rumor. If Vess is only a name over the door, which conjunct of the law has even begun to stand for him, and which are still empty spaces on the board?

deliberation.diagnosis: Only one prior declared figure is on record, so a three-turn figure rut is impossible.

transcript.text: Rule 6 needs three things: that Vess could have written it, that he had its source, and that his hand corrected the draft. Not one of those stands. 'CouldHaveWritten' wants a period on the paper and Vess in the ledger for that same period — I have neither. 'HadSourceOf' wants a quoted tune traced to walls he dwelled in — nothing on my board touches that. 'DraftCorrectedBy' wants physical evidence of his hand striking out and rewriting — the unsigned leaf tells me only that no name was signed, not whose pen moved. All three conjuncts are empty spaces.

meta.hypothesis: Vess composed the nocturne — but all three conjuncts of Rule 6 remain ungrounded for him; the hypothesis has no support yet on the board.

transcript.text: [The cover is drawn from the music desk, and the damp leaves are shown one by one to the gathered musicians. Every musician shown the leaves says the same: this is Vess's late manner — the suspensions, the long pedal, the way the bass walks.]

Style is a lantern, not a key. It lights the tempting door marked Vess, but it does not enter any chamber Rule 6 requires: capacity, source, correcting hand. Let the manner stand as pressure, not proof.

deliberation.diagnosis: The last two turns declared erotema and the draft declares erotema again, making a real three-turn figure rut.
deliberation.note: Leave off erotema this turn. Make the distinction without another guiding question, so the learner is not marched through the same device three times.

transcript.text: I will enter the style fact — the musicians said it plainly, and their words are on record. But I search all six rules for 'styleOf' and find it nowhere; it does not unlock a single conjunct. The board has one more leaf, and the chain is no longer than before.

meta.hypothesis: Vess composed the nocturne — but style is still not capacity, source, or correcting hand; all three conjuncts of Rule 6 remain empty for him.

transcript.text: [Rain works softly at the high panes while the uncovered leaves lie still beneath the lamps. No one reaches for the score; the room waits with its verdict hanging in the damp air.]
Then let the style verdict sit where it belongs. If Rule 6 cannot read style, what kind of fact must the room produce before any name can even become possible?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut: the last two turns were erotema then analogia, so the draft's erotema is not a third in a row.

transcript.text: Before any name can even become a candidate, the board needs a period: a watermark read against the stock ledger, or an ink batch traced to its mixing date — only then can Rule 1 or 2 yield 'writtenDuring'. Without a period, Rule 3 cannot fire, and 'couldHaveWritten' stays empty for everyone. Then the ledger must show a signature covering that same period, and the leaves must yield a quoted tune tied to walls someone dwelled in, and a correcting hand marked in the draft. Four kinds of physical fact — paper, ink, ledger, correction — none of which the room has yet set before me.

meta.hypothesis: Vess composed the nocturne — but all three conjuncts of Rule 6 remain ungrounded for him; the board cannot yet establish 'writtenDuring', 'atConservatoryDuring', 'hadSourceOf', or 'draftCorrectedBy' for any person.

transcript.text: [A clerk closes the outer doors against the rain, and the hall narrows to lamp-glass, wet wool, and the pale leaves on the desk. The verdict remains in the room without gaining weight.]

Take back the musicians' verdict: Vess's manner is on the leaves, but the law has no chamber for style. If style presses toward a name, which missing conjunct must answer first before pressure can become proof?

deliberation.diagnosis: The last two declared figures are not the same, so a third-turn figure rut has not formed.

transcript.text: The tutor names the right pressure point. Style bends toward Vess, but Rule 6 opens only from the left: 'couldHaveWritten' is the first conjunct, and it cannot exist without 'writtenDuring', which cannot exist without a watermark read against the stock ledger or an ink batch traced to its mixing date. Physical paper or physical ink must speak before any name — even a stylistically obvious one — can even stand in the dock. Style is still waiting outside the door.

meta.hypothesis: Vess composed the nocturne — but all three conjuncts of Rule 6 remain ungrounded for him; the board cannot establish 'writtenDuring' without a watermark (Rule 1) or ink batch (Rule 2), so 'couldHaveWritten' — the first and necessary conjunct — stays empty for everyone.

transcript.text: [The lamps burn lower, and their small reflections tremble in the dark gloss of the table. Beyond the closed doors, rain continues without argument, while the pale leaves remain exposed and unanswered.]
Then lift the leaves to the lamp. Every page gives the same sign: the heron watermark, Brell mill's mark. Style stands aside; paper has entered the law.

deliberation.diagnosis: The last two turns declared erotema and this draft declares erotema again, making a three-turn figure rut.
deliberation.note: Leave off erotema for this turn. Let the tutor speak without another question-shaped hinge.

transcript.text: The heron mark is on the leaves — that is one conjunct of Rule 1 now grounded. But Rule 1 requires a second fact: that heronStock was in the copy-room only during one period. The stock ledger has not yet told me when Brell's paper sat there and only there. Paper has entered the law; the period has not yet followed it in.

meta.hypothesis: Vess composed the nocturne — the watermark conjunct of Rule 1 is now on the board, but Rule 1 still needs stockUsedOnlyDuring heronStock ?period before writtenDuring can be derived; without that, couldHaveWritten stays empty for everyone, and Rules 3 and 6 cannot fire.

transcript.text: [The leaves are left beneath the lamp, their watermarks visible only when the clerk’s hand steadies the glass. No one touches the stock-books yet; the rain works at the shutters, and the room waits for paper to become time.]

Keep two columns apart: the musicians hear Vess's late manner, and the paper shows only the heron mark. Which column can the law use next, and what kind of missing fact would turn that mark into a season?

deliberation.diagnosis: The last two figures are mixed, so the draft's erotema is a return, not a three-turn rut.

transcript.text: The paper column is where the law can move — it already holds 'watermark nocturne heronStock'. The musicians' manner-column has no rule to stand on at all; style appears in none of the six rules. The missing fact is 'stockUsedOnlyDuring heronStock ?period': the stock ledger must show that Brell's heron paper sat in the copy-room during one period and no other. Only that entry converts a mill-mark into a season and lets Rule 1 fire.

meta.hypothesis: Vess composed the nocturne — the watermark conjunct of Rule 1 is on the board, but Rule 1 still needs stockUsedOnlyDuring heronStock ?period before writtenDuring can be derived; without that, couldHaveWritten stays empty for everyone, and Rules 3 and 6 cannot fire.

transcript.text: [The clerk closes the lamp’s shutter halfway, and the heron marks sink back into ordinary paper. Along the wall the unopened ledgers darken in their shelves, while rain ticks steadily into a basin set beneath the cracked ceiling.]
Bring the leaf back to the lamp before it fades from your case: what mark is actually on the nocturne, and what second kind of record must stand beside that mark before paper becomes time?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut: the last two turns are exemplum then erotema, and the draft would only make a two-turn erotema recurrence.

transcript.text: The mark on the nocturne is heronStock — Brell mill's heron, already entered at index 5. The second record needed is the stock ledger: it must show that heronStock sat in the copy-room during one period and no other, giving me stockUsedOnlyDuring heronStock ?period. Without that entry Rule 1 cannot fire. Two facts in hand; one still missing from the shelf.

meta.hypothesis: Vess composed the nocturne — the watermark conjunct of Rule 1 is on the board, but Rule 1 still needs stockUsedOnlyDuring heronStock ?period before writtenDuring can be derived; without that, couldHaveWritten stays empty for everyone, and Rules 3 and 6 cannot fire.

transcript.text: [The steward's stock-book is carried from the shelf and opened under the lamp. The steward's stock-book is plain: heron paper entered the copy-room in the winter of the flood, and the last of it was spent before the thaw. None before, none since.]

phase.name: The Dating
phase.intent: To turn the heron mark into time and let material proof begin to unsettle the hall's verdict.

transcript.text: The leaf says heron; the stock-book says flood winter only. Paper has crossed into time under Rule 1. Write the new line, and keep its boundary visible: it dates the work, but it names no hand.

deliberation.diagnosis: The draft would make erotema three turns in a row, so the tutor is in a figure rut.
deliberation.note: Leave off erotema for this turn. State the move as a plain threshold crossed, then let the learner supply the limit without another question-shaped cue.

transcript.text: Paper has become time. Rule 1 fires directly: watermark nocturne heronStock and stockUsedOnlyDuring heronStock floodWinter together yield writtenDuring nocturne floodWinter. The nocturne was written in the flood winter — the stock-book closes that door on every other season. That is a date, not a name; the hand is still missing.

meta.hypothesis: Vess composed the nocturne — Rule 1 has now yielded writtenDuring nocturne floodWinter, but couldHaveWritten still requires atConservatoryDuring ?x floodWinter (needing a ledger signature under Rule 4), and Rules 5 and 6 still want hadSourceOf and draftCorrectedBy for any person before the question can close.

transcript.text: [The stock-book remains open beneath the lamp, its ruled columns throwing narrow shadows across the nocturne leaves. Beyond the windows the rain slackens for a moment, and the hall listens to the quieter sound of water moving somewhere behind the walls.]
The paper has given you flood winter, but Rule 3 still asks for a person in that room during that winter. Which column on your board can name presence, and which column is still only manner?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut: the last two declared figures are erotema and anaphora, so erotema now does not make three in a row.

transcript.text: The column that can name presence is the copy-room ledger: Rule 4 says signedLedger ?x floodWinter yields atConservatoryDuring ?x floodWinter, which is the very fact Rule 3 needs. The column that is only manner is styleOf — index 4 on my board tells me how the music moves, not which body stood in which room. I have the date; I still lack the signature.

meta.hypothesis: Vess composed the nocturne — Rule 1 has yielded writtenDuring nocturne floodWinter, but couldHaveWritten still requires atConservatoryDuring ?x floodWinter (needing a ledger signature under Rule 4), and Rules 5 and 6 still want hadSourceOf and draftCorrectedBy for any person before the question can close.

transcript.text: [The copy-room ledger lies closed beside the open stock-book, its clasp dark with old tarnish. The lamp flame gutters once as damp air presses under the door, and every page on the table seems to wait for a hand that has not yet been called.]

Let the old style verdict stand where it belongs: manner can tempt the ear, but Rule 6 will not take manner for presence. You have flood winter on the page; what released fact on your board can put any named body in the conservatory during that winter?

deliberation.diagnosis: The last two declared figures are not the same device, so the draft's erotema is not a three-turn rut.

transcript.text: The tutor asks the right question, and my board answers it plainly: nothing. I have writtenDuring nocturne floodWinter, but no signedLedger fact for anyone — the ledger lies closed and its columns are blank on my board. Rule 4 cannot fire without a signature; Rule 3 cannot fire without Rule 4; no person is placed in that room. The ledger must be opened before the chain can continue.

meta.hypothesis: Vess composed the nocturne — Rule 1 has yielded writtenDuring nocturne floodWinter, but couldHaveWritten still requires atConservatoryDuring ?x floodWinter (needing a ledger signature under Rule 4), and Rules 5 and 6 still want hadSourceOf and draftCorrectedBy for any person before the question can close.

transcript.text: [A runner is shown in with the capital's concert bills, their wet edges pinned flat under glass. The clerk reads the line into the room: "The capital's concert bills run unbroken through the flood winter: Vess played the Esten halls every week of it, and did not return until the thaw."]

A concert bill is a locked door, not a signature. The paper fixes the nocturne to flood winter; the bills keep Vess in the capital through that same winter. His style remains only manner, while Rule 6's first conjunct has no room to stand.

deliberation.diagnosis: The last two turns declared erotema and the draft declares erotema again, making a three-turn figure rut.
deliberation.note: Leave off erotema this turn. State the pressure as a clean declarative turn rather than another question.

transcript.text: The concert bills place Vess in the capital through every week of flood winter. Awayness is not a rule-predicate, but its plain consequence is this: atConservatoryDuring vess floodWinter cannot hold, so Rule 3's first conjunct fails for him outright. The hypothesis that Vess composed the nocturne is no longer merely ungrounded — it is actively blocked. The question stands open; I need a name that did sign the ledger during flood winter.

[The concert bills remain under glass while the ledger waits beside them, unopened and heavy as a locked instrument case. Rain ticks in the chimney, and the clerks lower their voices around the table, as though the room itself has learned to distrust an easy name.]

The watermark named the stock; the stock-book named flood winter; the bills bar the easy name from that winter. If Rule 6 begins with could-have-written, what kind of released fact must next put some other named body in the conservatory then?

deliberation.diagnosis: The last two declared figures are erotema and analogia, and the draft moves to anaphora, so there is no three-turn figure rut.

transcript.text: The tutor names it exactly: Rule 4 is the gate. A signedLedger fact for some person during flood winter would fire Rule 4, yielding atConservatoryDuring; Rule 3 would then convert that into couldHaveWritten — the first conjunct Rule 6 demands. No new exhibits sit on the table; I cannot adopt what has not been offered. The question stays open until the ledger yields a name.

meta.hypothesis: Some unnamed person who signed the copy-room ledger during flood winter is the true composer — but no signedLedger fact for any such person has yet been entered on the table, so this remains entirely ungrounded conjecture.

transcript.text: [The unopened ledger is turned once so its spine faces the lamps, but no hand breaks the cord. Outside, the rain slackens to a fine hiss against the flooded panes, and every chair in the room seems angled toward the book.]
Before the cord breaks, call the night porter: he remembers the flood winter by its lamps, burning past midnight week upon week. And who kept them burning, by his memory? Liane, the archive girl.

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut: the last two declared figures were analogia and anaphora, and the draft shifts to erotema rather than repeating a third time.

transcript.text: The porter's word is testimony, not a ledger cord — but the exhibit arrives as atConservatoryDuring directly, and I take what the table gives me. Rule 3 fires at once: writtenDuring nocturne floodWinter is already on record, and atConservatoryDuring liane floodWinter now joins it, so couldHaveWritten liane nocturne follows. One conjunct of Rule 6 is in hand. Two remain entirely empty: hadSourceOf and draftCorrectedBy. Liane is a name, not yet an answer.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — but couldHaveWritten is only the first conjunct of Rule 6; hadSourceOf liane nocturne and draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane have no ground yet, and without both, Rule 6 cannot fire.

transcript.text: [The porter's chair is drawn back from the table, leaving a small dark scrape on the boards. The ledger cord lies untouched beneath the lamp, and the rain gathers again at the window in a patient, indistinct murmur.]

Set the lamp back on the heron mark: the mark names the stock, the stock-book names flood winter, the porter puts Liane in that winter. What does Rule 3 now permit you to say, and which two doors of Rule 6 are still shut?

deliberation.diagnosis: The last two figures are not the same, so the draft's anaphora does not make a three-turn rut.

transcript.text: Rule 3 permits exactly one step: writtenDuring nocturne floodWinter sits on record, atConservatoryDuring liane floodWinter sits on the board, so couldHaveWritten liane nocturne follows — I voice it now and enter it. That is one conjunct of Rule 6. The two doors still shut are hadSourceOf liane nocturne and draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane; no fact on the board touches either. The lamp is back on the heron mark, but two seals remain unbroken.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — but couldHaveWritten is only the first conjunct of Rule 6; hadSourceOf liane nocturne and draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane have no ground yet, and without both, Rule 6 cannot fire.

transcript.text: [The lamp is lowered until the heron mark fades into the grain of the page, and the ledger cord throws a thin shadow across the table. In the pause, the flooded archive answers with one slow drip from somewhere beyond the stacks.]
One door has opened: capacity. Before any new witness speaks, what kind of fact would open the source-door under Rule 5, and why is capacity alone still insufficient for Rule 6?

deliberation.diagnosis: The last two declared figures are mixed, so the draft's erotema does not make three in a row.

transcript.text: Rule 5 needs three things together: that the nocturne quotes some melody, that the melody was heard only within one particular place, and that Liane dwelled there — all three, or hadSourceOf never fires. Capacity means she could have held the pen; source means she had the raw material to put on the page; correcting hand means the draft shows her striking out and rewriting, not merely copying fair. Rule 6 demands all three conjuncts simultaneously — one open door leaves two still sealed, and a sealed door is no different from a locked one.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — but couldHaveWritten is only the first conjunct of Rule 6; hadSourceOf liane nocturne and draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane have no ground yet, and without both, Rule 6 cannot fire.

transcript.text: [The room settles around the three sealed doors as if around covered instruments. Rain beads on the high windows, and the unanswered nocturne lies between the lamp and the ledger, silent under its unsigned page.]

phase.name: The Cradle-Song
phase.intent: To change the room from dating and alibi to the pressure of source, while keeping the next door unopened until the stage receives it.

transcript.text: Let the page sound once, not as rumor but as exhibit: under the right hand's figures, the nocturne carries an older tune entire, a cradle-song kept like a small flame in the middle voice. If Rule 5 waits on source, what has this playing opened, and what two further facts would still have to meet it?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut: the last two declared figures are mixed, and the draft only repeats the immediately prior erotema once.

transcript.text: The playing enters one conjunct: quotes nocturne cradleSong now sits on the board. Rule 5 reads it, but cannot move — it still needs heardOnlyWithin cradleSong for some named place, and dwelledIn liane for that same place. Both are absent. Two facts must meet this new leaf before hadSourceOf fires, and draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane remains untouched by any of this.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — couldHaveWritten is grounded, quotes nocturne cradleSong is now entered, but Rule 5 still wants heardOnlyWithin cradleSong ?place and dwelledIn liane ?place before hadSourceOf yields, and draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has no ground at all; Rule 6 cannot fire.

transcript.text: [The cradle-song's last vibration seems to remain under the closed lid of the keyboard, though no hand returns to it. Outside, rain slides down the glass in separate threads, and the table holds its exhibits without yielding another scrap.]
The quote is a key, not yet a hand on the key. It proves the nocturne carries the cradle-song; Rule 5 still waits for the song's closed house and for a person shown to have lived inside that house.

deliberation.diagnosis: The last two turns used erotema and this draft declares erotema again, making a three-turn figure rut.
deliberation.note: Leave off erotema for this turn. State the distinction directly or move by another figure, but do not shape the line as another question.

transcript.text: Nothing new sits on the table; the board has not moved. The quote is entered and on the record, but it is one conjunct lying alone — Rule 5 cannot stir without heardOnlyWithin and dwelledIn to meet it. The source-door and the correcting-hand door are both still sealed. I hold the hypothesis and wait for the archive to offer the remaining leaves.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — couldHaveWritten is grounded, quotes nocturne cradleSong is entered, but Rule 5 still wants heardOnlyWithin cradleSong ?place and dwelledIn liane ?place before hadSourceOf yields, and draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has no ground at all; Rule 6 cannot fire.

transcript.text: [The archivist's empty chair creaks once as the damp wood settles. The lamp is turned lower, making the unsigned page shine at its edges while the rest of the room gathers into a patient dark.]

Before the next leaf arrives, set the old ones in order again: the heron stock fixed the nocturne to the flood winter, and the porter put Liane in the conservatory then. With only the cradle-song now added, which door has opened, and which door is still only named, not shown?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut: the last two declared figures were erotema and analogia, and the draft shifts to anaphora rather than repeating either into a third turn.

transcript.text: The heron stock and the ledger together opened one door: couldHaveWritten liane nocturne stands derived, Rule 6's first conjunct settled. The cradle-song quote is entered but it is only the first leaf of Rule 5 — without heardOnlyWithin and dwelledIn it cannot yield hadSourceOf, so that door is only named, not opened. draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has not been named at all — that third door has not even a handle showing.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — couldHaveWritten is grounded, quotes nocturne cradleSong is entered, but Rule 5 still wants heardOnlyWithin cradleSong ?place and dwelledIn liane ?place before hadSourceOf yields, and draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has no ground at all; Rule 6 cannot fire.

transcript.text: [Mistress Odile is shown into the lamplit room, her shawl still dark from the rain, and the nocturne is played only as far as the hidden tune. Mistress Odile knows the tune at one hearing: the Vara cradle-song, never printed, never performed, sung only within that house's walls.]
Now the quote has a house: the cradle-song belongs within Vara walls, not the public hall. Under Rule 5, what still must be shown before any person can be said to have had the nocturne's source?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut: the last two declared figures were analogia and anaphora, and the draft shifts to erotema.

transcript.text: The house is now named — varaHouse, and the cradle-song never left its walls. Rule 5 has two of its three leaves: the quote, and the closed house. The third leaf is still missing: I need dwelledIn ?x varaHouse before hadSourceOf can be yielded for any person. The door is labelled now, but no resident has yet been shown to me.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — couldHaveWritten is grounded, quotes nocturne cradleSong and heardOnlyWithin cradleSong varaHouse are now both on the board, but Rule 5 still wants dwelledIn liane varaHouse before hadSourceOf yields, and draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has no ground at all; Rule 6 cannot fire.

transcript.text: [Rain works at the high windows until the panes blur into black mirrors. On the table, the unsigned nocturne lies beside the closed testimony book, and no hand reaches for either.]
The house has been named, but no dweller has yet crossed its threshold in evidence. Which conjunct of Rule 5 is still empty, and why does that keep Rule 6 from moving beyond capacity?

deliberation.diagnosis: The last two declared figures are not the same, so the draft's erotema does not make a three-turn figure rut.

transcript.text: Rule 5's third conjunct is the empty one: dwelledIn ?x varaHouse has no fact behind it. Without it, hadSourceOf cannot be derived for any person — so Rule 6 stalls after its first conjunct, couldHaveWritten liane nocturne, and cannot advance to the second. draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane is also absent, so even if a dweller were shown, Rule 6 would still want a third leaf.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — couldHaveWritten is grounded, quotes nocturne cradleSong and heardOnlyWithin cradleSong varaHouse are both on the board, but Rule 5 still wants dwelledIn liane varaHouse before hadSourceOf yields, and draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has no ground at all; Rule 6 cannot fire.

transcript.text: [The lamplight gutters and steadies, throwing the nocturne's damp edges into brief relief. The room waits around the closed testimony book, its silence sharpened by the rain.]

Style is a costume, not a key: it can make the room look toward Vess, but it cannot open capacity, source, or correcting hand. The concert bills have closed the winter room to him; the law still asks for its three locks, not a likeness.

deliberation.diagnosis: The draft would make erotema the declared figure for a third consecutive turn, so the manner has become a rut.
deliberation.note: Leave off erotema this turn. Do not press by question; move by another device.

transcript.text: No new exhibit has crossed the threshold this turn. The board stands as it stood: dwelledIn liane varaHouse is still absent, so Rule 5 cannot yield hadSourceOf. And draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has never appeared at all. Two locks remain shut; Rule 6 cannot fire.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — couldHaveWritten is grounded, quotes nocturne cradleSong and heardOnlyWithin cradleSong varaHouse are both on the board, but Rule 5 still wants dwelledIn liane varaHouse before hadSourceOf yields, and draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has no ground at all; Rule 6 cannot fire.

transcript.text: [The testimony book is opened at last, and a patron's note is read into the wet hush: "And yet — Vess summered at the Vara house for years, the patron's own guest; whatever was sung under that roof, he will have heard." The page is left flat beneath the lamp, its ink dark against the swollen paper.]
The guest-note may open the Vara song to Vess, but does it open the flood-winter copy-room to him? Name which conjunct of Rule 6 it can help, and which conjunct the concert bills still refuse.

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut: the last two turns were erotema then analogia, and the draft's erotema does not make three in a row.

transcript.text: The guest-note opens Rule 5 for Vess at last: dwelledIn vess varaHouse joins quotes nocturne cradleSong and heardOnlyWithin cradleSong varaHouse, and hadSourceOf vess nocturne follows. That is Rule 6's second conjunct — granted. But the concert bills nailed awayDuring vess floodWinter to the board long ago: Rule 3 cannot yield couldHaveWritten vess nocturne without atConservatoryDuring vess floodWinter, and the bills refuse that entirely. So Vess gains the second lock but the first stays bolted — Rule 6 cannot fire for him.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — couldHaveWritten liane nocturne is grounded; but dwelledIn liane varaHouse has never appeared, so hadSourceOf liane nocturne cannot be derived, and draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has no ground at all; Rule 6 still cannot fire for anyone.

transcript.text: [The patron's page remains under the lamp while rain works softly at the high windows. No one reaches for the nocturne; the room holds its breath between a song that can travel and a winter room that cannot.]

Bring the stock-book back under the lamp: if the heron paper confines the nocturne to the flood winter, what do the concert bills do to Vess's first lock? The Vara song may give him source; can source replace presence?

deliberation.diagnosis: The last two declared figures are mixed, so the draft's erotema is not a three-turn rut.

transcript.text: Source cannot replace presence — the rules do not allow it. Rule 6 demands couldHaveWritten as its first conjunct, and that conjunct flows only through Rule 3, which requires atConservatoryDuring vess floodWinter. The concert bills planted awayDuring vess floodWinter on this board; that fact seals the first lock for Vess permanently. hadSourceOf vess nocturne is now derived, but it cannot substitute for a conjunct it does not touch.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — couldHaveWritten liane nocturne is grounded, but dwelledIn liane varaHouse has never appeared so hadSourceOf liane nocturne cannot derive, and draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has no ground at all; Rule 6 still cannot fire for anyone.

transcript.text: [The lamp gutters once, then steadies, throwing the same three documents into a longer, thinner light. Outside, the rain slackens to a patient ticking from the eaves, and the witnesses wait without speaking.]

Then keep the two cases separate on the board: for Vess, source is open but presence is closed; for Liane, presence is open but source is not yet complete. Which missing conjunct would change Liane's case from a guess into a lawful claim?

deliberation.diagnosis: The last two turns used erotema, but the draft shifts to anaphora, so there is no three-turn figure rut.

transcript.text: Rule 6 still wants two conjuncts from Liane: hadSourceOf liane nocturne and draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane. The first unlocks through Rule 5 — but only if dwelledIn liane varaHouse arrives; that fact has never appeared on the board. The second needs direct evidence of her correcting hand on the draft — also absent. Either alone is insufficient; both must be grounded before Rule 6 can fire.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — couldHaveWritten liane nocturne is grounded, but dwelledIn liane varaHouse has never appeared so hadSourceOf liane nocturne cannot derive, and draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has no ground at all; Rule 6 still cannot fire for anyone.

transcript.text: [The rain thins until the archive roof answers only with scattered drops. Beneath the lamp, the papers lie in their separate pools of light, each refusing to cross the gap for the others.]

Mistress Odile adds one more memory to the room: Liane was the music-master's orphan, taken into the Vara house as ward, raised under that roof and under its songs. If Rule 5 already holds the cradle-song inside those walls, what does this do to Liane's source conjunct?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut: the last two declared figures are mixed, and the draft does not make three of one device.

transcript.text: dwelledIn liane varaHouse lands on the board at last. Rule 5 can now fire: quotes nocturne cradleSong, heardOnlyWithin cradleSong varaHouse, dwelledIn liane varaHouse — hadSourceOf liane nocturne is derived. Two of Rule 6's three conjuncts are grounded for Liane. The third — draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane — has never appeared; Rule 6 still cannot fire.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — couldHaveWritten liane nocturne and hadSourceOf liane nocturne are both now grounded, but draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has no ground at all; Rule 6 cannot fire until the correcting hand is evidenced.

transcript.text: [The tutor's chalk rests beside the board, untouched, while the last derived line dries into the dust. The lamp throws its circle no wider than before, and the room holds its breath around the empty place where a correcting hand would have to appear.]

Good: the source conjunct now stands on the board, not as rumor but by Rule 5. If Rule 6 asks for three locks, which two are actually shut for Liane, and what kind of evidence must the third still be?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut: the last two turns were anaphora then erotema, so this draft's erotema is only a repeat of one turn, not three in a row.

transcript.text: Two locks are shut: couldHaveWritten liane nocturne stands by Rules 3 and 4; hadSourceOf liane nocturne stands by Rule 5. The third lock — draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane — is still open. It cannot be derived; it must be read from the draft itself: ink struck through, notes rewritten, in a hand the archive can identify as Liane's.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — couldHaveWritten liane nocturne and hadSourceOf liane nocturne are both grounded, but draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has no ground at all; Rule 6 cannot fire until the correcting hand is evidenced by physical examination of the draft.

transcript.text: [The archivist lowers the glass back over the draft without touching the page. Outside, the last rainwater threads down the high windows, and every face in the room turns toward the ink as if waiting for it to move.]

phase.name: The Hand
phase.intent: To shift the room from source and presence to the physical draft, where the remaining claim must be won by correction and recognition.

transcript.text: Keep the glass on the draft; keep Rule 6 on the board; keep the empty square empty. Two conjuncts stand for Liane: capacity and source. The board may mark a candidate, not a composer, until the correcting hand is staged.

deliberation.diagnosis: The last two turns declared erotema and this draft declares erotema again, so the tutor is entering a three-turn figure rut.
deliberation.note: Leave off erotema for this turn. Make the pressure declarative or procedural instead of casting it as another question.

transcript.text: No new exhibits on the table; the board stands unchanged. Two locks are shut for Liane — capacity and source — and both are grounded. The third lock, the correcting hand, still has no exhibit to speak to it. Until the draft yields a physical mark attributable to Liane, Rule 6 cannot fire and the question stays open.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — couldHaveWritten liane nocturne and hadSourceOf liane nocturne are both grounded, but draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has no ground at all; Rule 6 cannot fire until the correcting hand is evidenced by physical examination of the draft.

transcript.text: [The glass remains over the draft, bright with the lamp's reflection. No one reaches for it; the room listens to the rain and to the chalkboard's unfinished silence.]

Then let the ledger itself come under the lamp: its winter pages carry Liane's initials line after line, signed through the whole flood winter. If Rule 4 trusts that book, what does this signature make firmer about the first lock, and what does it still leave untouched?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut: the last two declared figures are mixed, and the draft does not make three of one device in a row.

transcript.text: I adopt the ledger entry. Rule 4 now derives atConservatoryDuring liane floodWinter independently — the first lock had already shut, but it now has a second bolt: the raw board fact at index 6 and Rule 4 working from the signature together. What the ledger cannot touch is the third lock: it says only where Liane stood, not whose hand struck out the draft.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — couldHaveWritten liane nocturne and hadSourceOf liane nocturne are both grounded, but draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has no ground at all; Rule 6 cannot fire until the correcting hand is evidenced by physical examination of the draft.

transcript.text: [The ledger stays open beneath the lamp, its ruled columns paling at the edges of the light. Beside it the covered draft waits, and the room holds to the narrow space between a name repeatedly written and a hand not yet proved.]
The ledger gives a name in the room, not a hand on the draft. It can strengthen the first conjunct; it cannot write the third. Which square of Rule 6 remains deliberately blank?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut: the last two turns alternate anaphora and erotema, and the draft's anaphora is not a third repetition.

transcript.text: The blank square is draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane — the third conjunct of Rule 6, and the only one still empty. The ledger fills no part of it: a signature on ruled columns is not a strikethrough on a manuscript. Two locks shut, one lock open; the board has not moved.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — couldHaveWritten liane nocturne and hadSourceOf liane nocturne are both grounded, but draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has no ground at all; Rule 6 cannot fire until the correcting hand is evidenced by physical examination of the draft.

transcript.text: [The lamp gutters once and steadies, leaving the draft and ledger in their separate pools of light. The rain presses at the windows while the empty square on the board remains untouched.]

Do not let the repeated name do work the rule assigns to a different kind of mark. If capacity and source are already grounded, what sort of exhibit, not what answer, could lawfully close the third conjunct?

deliberation.diagnosis: The last two turns do not share the draft's declared device, so there is no three-turn figure rut.

transcript.text: The kind of exhibit that could close it: a physical examination of the draft itself, confirming that the strikethroughs and rewrites are in Liane's hand — perhaps a handwriting comparison against her known copies. No such finding is yet on the table. The third conjunct names a correcting hand, and only a mark on paper can name it.

meta.hypothesis: Liane composed the nocturne — couldHaveWritten liane nocturne and hadSourceOf liane nocturne are both grounded, but draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane has no ground at all; Rule 6 cannot fire until a handwriting examination or equivalent physical exhibit establishes whose hand struck out and rewrote the draft.

transcript.text: [The cover is lifted from the draft and the examiner sets it beside the ledger entries. Set beside her ledger entries, the draft's corrections show the same hand: the strokes that cross out and rewrite are Liane's.]
Now the third square has a mark on paper, not a rumor in the air. Take Rule 6 slowly: capacity, source, correcting hand — what does each conjunct now yield? And therefore...

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut: the last two turns declared anaphora and erotema, and this draft declares aposiopesis.

transcript.text: The third conjunct lands on the board. Rule 6 now has all three squares filled for Liane: capacity, source, correcting hand. The rules close without remainder. The nocturne is Liane's.
[For a moment no one touches the papers. Rainwater threads down the tall panes, and the lamp holds its narrow circle over the board where the last square has stopped being empty.]

Capacity from winter presence; source from the house-song; correcting hand from the draft beside the ledger. If those three stand, Rule 6 has no fourth door to open. Let the record take the sentence you have earned.

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut: the last two turns used erotema and aposiopesis, and the draft's anaphora does not make three of one device.

transcript.text: Board item 12 closes the last open square. Rule 6 demands capacity, source, and correcting hand — couldHaveWritten liane nocturne, hadSourceOf liane nocturne, draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane: all three stand grounded. The rules close without remainder. The nocturne is Liane's.

[The examiner leaves the two leaves where they lie, neither separating them nor folding them away. In the wet hush of the archive, the board waits under the lamp as if the ink itself has become the room's last witness.]
Then seal it conjunct by conjunct, as the archive would: not who seems, not who is famed, but who can, who has, who corrected. Which name survives all three tests?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut: the last two turns were aposiopesis and anaphora, and this draft only makes a second nearby anaphora, not a third.

transcript.text: Conjunct one: signedLedger liane floodWinter yields atConservatoryDuring by Rule 4; writtenDuring nocturne floodWinter with that yields couldHaveWritten liane nocturne by Rule 3. Conjunct two: quotes, heardOnlyWithin, dwelledIn — all three on the board — yield hadSourceOf liane nocturne by Rule 5. Conjunct three: draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane stands at board item 12, grounded by physical examination of the hand. Rule 6 has no fourth door; the name that survives all three tests is Liane.
[The lamp is turned a fraction lower, and the papers seem to draw closer together in its diminished light. Outside the flooded archive, the rain keeps its patient measure against the glass.]
Style may bow from the doorway; it cannot sign the ledger, carry the house-song, and correct the draft. When a likeness stands against three conjuncts, what does the archive file: likeness, or proof?

deliberation.diagnosis: The last two turns declared anaphora and the draft declares anaphora again, making a three-turn figure rut.
deliberation.note: Leave off anaphora now. Let the line move by a different pressure rather than another repeated frame.

transcript.text: The archive files proof. Style is a likeness — it cannot be cross-examined. The three conjuncts are facts, not resemblances. Rule 6 closes on liane: capacity grounded, source grounded, correcting hand grounded. I enter the binding answer.

[The clerk draws the blotter over the margin of the board and then stops, leaving the last line uncovered. The archive holds its breath around the exposed name, while rainwater ticks from a high crack into the basin below.]

Before the clerk dries it, set the first stone back under the lamp: heron stock, flood winter. From that winter, through the ledger, through the house-song, through the correcting hand — what sentence may the archive write?

deliberation.diagnosis: The recent sequence is mixed, so the declared anaphora does not make three in a row.

transcript.text: From heron stock: Rule 1 yields writtenDuring nocturne floodWinter. From the ledger: Rule 4 yields atConservatoryDuring liane floodWinter, and Rule 3 closes to couldHaveWritten liane nocturne. From cradleSong heard only within varaHouse, where liane dwelled: Rule 5 yields hadSourceOf liane nocturne. From the correcting hand on the draft: draftCorrectedBy nocturne liane stands grounded. Rule 6 closes: the archive writes — Liane composed the nocturne.
